title: [BUS] Add InterfaceType to PortDefinition and update WiringLoader validation
body:
## Objective
Extend PortDefinition with an interface_type field. Update WiringLoader to validate interface type compatibility between connected ports.

## Design
```python
class InterfaceType(enum.Enum):
    FLOAT      = 'float'       # default, backward compatible
    MIL1553_BC = 'mil1553_bc'  # 1553 Bus Controller
    MIL1553_RT = 'mil1553_rt'  # 1553 Remote Terminal
    SPACEWIRE  = 'spacewire'   # SpaceWire node
    CAN        = 'can'         # CAN node
    UART       = 'uart'        # UART
    ANALOG     = 'analog'      # analog signal
    DIGITAL    = 'digital'     # digital signal
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- InterfaceType enum added to equipment.py
- PortDefinition.interface_type field added (default: FLOAT)
- WiringLoader raises WiringLoadError when interface types are incompatible
- All existing tests pass (FLOAT ports are backward compatible)
- mypy strict clean

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement Bus abstract base class extending Equipment
body:
## Objective
Define the Bus abstract base class. A Bus is an Equipment with typed ports on both sides and built-in fault injection support.

## Design
```python
class Bus(Equipment):
    def inject_fault(self, fault: BusFault) -> None: ...
    def clear_faults(self) -> None: ...
    def active_faults(self) -> list[BusFault]: ...
```

## BusFault schema
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BusFault:
    fault_type: FaultType  # NO_RESPONSE, LATE_RESPONSE, BAD_PARITY, BUS_ERROR
    target: str            # equipment_id or 'all'
    duration_s: float      # 0.0 = permanent
    injected_at: float     # simulation time
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bus extends Equipment
- inject_fault(), clear_faults(), active_faults() implemented
- Time-limited faults expire automatically each tick
- Fault state readable from ParameterStore as bus.{bus_id}.fault.{target}
- Unit tests cover fault injection, expiry, clearing

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement MIL-STD-1553 Bus adapter
body:
## Objective
Implement a MIL-STD-1553 bus adapter as a Bus Equipment.

## Design
- Mil1553Bus extends Bus
- IN port: bc_in (type: MIL1553_BC) — receives from OBC BC
- OUT ports: rt1_out through rt30_out (type: MIL1553_RT)
- Dual redundant bus (A/B) with automatic switchover on BUS_ERROR fault
- Subaddress-to-parameter mapping defined in wiring YAML
- Broadcast command support (RT address 31)

## Wiring YAML extension
```yaml
connections:
  - from: obc.m1553_bc_out
    to:   platform_bus.bc_in
    interface: MIL1553_BC
  - from: platform_bus.rt5_out
    to:   rw1.m1553_rt_in
    interface: MIL1553_RT
    rt_address: 5
    subaddress_map:
      - sa: 1
        parameter: aocs.rw1.torque_cmd
      - sa: 2
        parameter: aocs.rw1.speed
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mil1553Bus routes BC commands to correct RT based on RT address
- Subaddress mapping translates parameters correctly
- Dual bus A/B supported with switchover on BUS_ERROR
- Broadcast commands delivered to all RTs
- Unit tests cover routing, subaddress mapping, broadcast

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement OBC NativeEquipment as 1553 Bus Controller
body:
## Objective
Implement a simple OBC model as NativeEquipment acting as 1553 Bus Controller for the M6 demo scenario.

## Design
- ObcEquipment extends NativeEquipment
- Offers m1553_bc_out port (type: MIL1553_BC)
- Reads CommandStore for telecommands to forward onto bus
- Writes received telemetry to ParameterStore
- Simple FDIR: detects RT timeout, switches to redundant bus

## Acceptance Criteria
- OBC forwards TCs to correct RT address via 1553 bus
- OBC detects NO_RESPONSE fault and flags obc.fdir.rt{n}_fault
- OBC switches bus A->B on persistent fault
- Unit tests cover nominal commanding and FDIR response

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement ReactionWheel as 1553 Remote Terminal
body:
## Objective
Extend the ReactionWheel model to implement the 1553 RT interface, enabling it to receive commands and send telemetry over the simulated 1553 bus.

## Design
- RwEquipment extends NativeEquipment
- Offers m1553_rt_in port (type: MIL1553_RT)
- Receives torque commands via SA1, sends speed telemetry via SA2
- Physics: speed increases with torque, decays without power

## Acceptance Criteria
- RW receives torque command from OBC via 1553 bus
- RW sends speed telemetry back to OBC via 1553 bus
- RW does not respond when NO_RESPONSE fault is active
- Unit tests cover nominal operation and fault response

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038, SVF-DEV-066

title: [BUS] Write 1553 validation and FDIR test procedures
body:
## Objective
Write end-to-end test procedures demonstrating OBC commanding a RW over simulated 1553 bus including FDIR scenarios.

## Test Cases
- TC-1553-001: OBC commands RW torque via 1553, speed increases
- TC-1553-002: OBC reads RW speed telemetry via 1553
- TC-1553-003: NO_RESPONSE fault injected, OBC detects within 2 frames
- TC-1553-004: OBC switches to redundant bus after persistent fault
- TC-1553-005: Broadcast command delivered to all RTs

## Acceptance Criteria
- All test procedures use svf_session fixture
- Fault injection via svf_command_schedule
- All tests pass
- Campaign YAML committed: campaigns/1553_validation.yaml
- HTML report generated cleanly

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement SpaceWire Bus adapter with RMAP
body:
## Objective
Implement a SpaceWire bus adapter with RMAP (Remote Memory Access Protocol) support.

## Design
- SpwBus extends Bus
- Router + node topology
- RMAP read/write transactions
- Logical address routing
- SpW node port type for equipment

## Acceptance Criteria
- SpwBus routes RMAP transactions to correct node by logical address
- Read and write RMAP transactions supported
- Error injection: link error, invalid address, RMAP error codes
- Integration test with payload mass memory model

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038

title: [BUS] Implement CAN Bus adapter (ECSS)
body:
## Objective
Implement a CAN bus adapter following ECSS CAN conventions.

## Design
- CanBus extends Bus
- CAN 2.0B frame structure
- Standard and extended identifiers
- ECSS CAN node addressing

## Acceptance Criteria
- CanBus routes messages by CAN identifier
- Standard and extended frame formats supported
- Error injection: bus-off, error passive, ACK error
- Integration test with a simple CAN-connected equipment

## Implements
SVF-DEV-038
